<user_query>
=== PHASE 6 — FIX 3 APPROVED BUGS (Scenario Contract approved) ===

Context: Phase 5 diagnosis confirmed 3 root causes with live evidence.
Scope decisions made by the developer:

ValidationBehavior fix applies ONLY to Customer.Api / Customer.Application
(do NOT touch Admin.Api or Admin.Application in this task — out of scope,
to be reviewed separately later)
Bug 2 (block) fix: ONLY strategy A (reactivate soft-deleted row) —
strategy B (filtered unique index / migration) is explicitly OUT of scope
for today
Bug 3 fix (narrowing catch blocks) goes in a SEPARATE commit from the
ValidationBehavior fix (Bug 1), per Section 30.7 one-logical-change rule
Before writing anything, state out loud per GEMINI.md Section 27/22:
Sections I will read: [list them]
Files I will MODIFY: [exact paths]
Assumptions I am making: [list any]

=== FIX 1 — ValidationBehavior null-cast bug (Customer only) ===

File: Customer/Core/Application/Behaviors/ValidationBehavior.cs

Read the FULL file first. The current bug:

text

Result error = Error.AccessUnAuthorized("", sb.ToString());
return error as TResponse;
This works when TResponse is exactly Result (non-generic), but returns
null when TResponse is Result<T> for any T, because Result cannot be
cast to Result<T> via as.

Fix approach: detect whether TResponse is Result or a closed generic
Result<T>, and construct the correctly-typed failure value using
reflection or a type-check pattern, OR (preferred, simpler, and idiomatic
per this codebase's Result pattern) leverage the existing implicit
conversion operators already defined on Result<TValue>:

csharp

public static implicit operator Result<TValue>(Error error) => new(error);
Since Result<TValue> already has public static implicit operator Result<TValue>(Error error), the cleanest fix is to build the failure at
the Error level and let each concrete Result<T> do its own implicit
conversion — but this requires knowing T at compile time, which the open
generic behavior does not have directly. Investigate and implement ONE of
these two idiomatic solutions (pick whichever fits this codebase's existing
patterns best after reading the file, and justify your choice):

Option 1 (reflection-based, generic-safe):

csharp

if (typeof(TResponse) == typeof(Result))
{
    return (TResponse)(object)Result.Failure(error);
}
if (typeof(TResponse).IsGenericType &&
    typeof(TResponse).GetGenericTypeDefinition() == typeof(Result<>))
{
    var failureMethod = typeof(Result<>)
        .MakeGenericType(typeof(TResponse).GetGenericArguments()[0])
        .GetMethod(nameof(Result.Failure), new[] { typeof(Error) });
    return (TResponse)failureMethod!.Invoke(null, new object[] { error })!;
}
// fallback: log a warning and return default — should never hit this
Option 2 (if this codebase already has a simpler established pattern for
this exact problem elsewhere — search for it first before assuming Option
1 is required; if found, prefer consistency with existing code).

Implement the fix. Preserve every other line of this file exactly (do not
refactor logging, do not change the validator collection/execution logic).

=== FIX 2 — Block reactivation (strategy A only) ===

File: Customer/Core/Application/Features/App/Chat/Users/Commands/BlockChatUser/BlockChatUserCommandHandler.cs

Read the FULL file first. Current logic queries with the default (soft-
delete-filtered) query, so it never sees a soft-deleted ChatBlock row for
the same (BlockerUserId, BlockedUserId) pair, then tries to INSERT a new
row and hits the unique index violation because the old (soft-deleted) row
physically still exists.

Fix: before inserting, check BOTH the active AND soft-deleted state:

csharp

var existingBlock = await context.ChatBlocks
    .IgnoreQueryFilters()
    .FirstOrDefaultAsync(b =>
        b.BlockerUserId == currentUserId &&
        b.BlockedUserId == request.TargetUserId,
        cancellationToken);

if (existingBlock is not null)
{
    if (!existingBlock.IsDeleted)
        return ChatErrors.AlreadyBlocked; // active block already exists

    // Reactivate the soft-deleted row instead of inserting a duplicate
    existingBlock.IsDeleted = false;
    existingBlock.DeleteBy = null;
    existingBlock.DeleteDate = null;
    existingBlock.BlockedAt = DateTime.UtcNow; // or use context.DateTime() per Section 26.6 pattern if that's the established helper
}
else
{
    context.ChatBlocks.Add(new ChatBlock
    {
        BlockerUserId = currentUserId,
        BlockedUserId = request.TargetUserId,
        BlockedAt = DateTime.UtcNow
    });
}

await context.SaveChangesAsync(cancellationToken);
Verify the exact property names for soft-delete fields on the base Entity
class (DeleteBy, DeleteDate, IsDeleted per Section 3 of GEMINI.md) and match
them exactly — do not guess field names, confirm against the actual Entity
base class. Also verify whether this project's AuditableEntityInterceptor
auto-clears DeleteBy/DeleteDate on reactivation (EntityState.Modified) or
whether you must clear them manually here — read the interceptor code
(already quoted in the Phase 5 report) to confirm, and only set what isn't
already handled automatically.

Do NOT implement strategy B (filtered unique index / migration) — leave
ChatBlockConfiguration.cs untouched.

Do NOT touch UnblockChatUserCommandHandler.cs in this fix — the hard
Remove() there stays as-is for today; it's what makes the soft-delete
ghost row exist in the first place, and reactivation (this fix) correctly
handles that ghost row without needing to change the unblock behavior.

=== FIX 3 — Narrow catch blocks masking validation errors (SEPARATE commit) ===

Files to modify:

Customer/Api/EndPoint/Chat/CreateGroupEndPoint.cs
Customer/Api/EndPoint/Chat/CreateChannelEndPoint.cs
Customer/Api/EndPoint/Chat/SendChatMessageEndPoint.cs
Customer/Api/EndPoint/Chat/UploadChatFileEndPoint.cs
Read each FULL file first. Current pattern wraps the ENTIRE handler body
(including sender.Send(...) and the res.IsSuccess check) inside one
try/catch that returns a generic hardcoded Persian message on ANY exception,
including cases where res ends up null due to Bug 1 (which Fix 1 above
resolves) — but this endpoint-level catch will still mask GENUINE unexpected
exceptions (e.g. a real file I/O error during avatar upload) with an
uninformative message, which is bad practice independent of Bug 1.

Fix: narrow the try/catch to wrap ONLY the code that can genuinely throw
non-Result exceptions (e.g. multipart form parsing: reading IFormCollection,
splitting comma-separated ID strings, file stream operations for avatar/
attachment uploads) — NOT the sender.Send(...) + res.IsSuccess check
block, since MediatR requests should now (after Fix 1) always return a
proper Result/Result<T>, never throw, and never be null.

Example restructure pattern (adapt exactly to each file's actual structure
after reading it — do not assume identical shape across all 4 files):

csharp

List<int> memberUserIds;
try
{
    memberUserIds = ParseMemberUserIds(form["memberUserIds"]);
    // any other form-parsing-specific logic that could throw
}
catch (Exception ex)
{
    Log.Error(ex, "CreateGroupConversation - form parsing failed");
    return Results.Problem("خطا در پردازش اطلاعات فرم", statusCode: 400);
}

var res = await sender.Send(
    new CreateGroupConversationCommand(name, avatarUrl, memberUserIds),
    cancellationToken);

if (res.IsSuccess)
    return Results.Ok(res.Value);

return Results.Problem(res!.Error!.Description, statusCode: 400);
Keep each endpoint's existing conflict-status-code logic (e.g.
CreateChannelEndPoint's ErrorType.Conflict → 409 check) intact, just move
it outside the narrowed try/catch.

Update the generic error message used in the narrowed catch to be more
specific to form/file parsing, e.g. "خطا در پردازش اطلاعات فرم" instead of
the misleadingly broad "خطا در ایجاد گروه" (since a form-parsing failure is
different from an "unable to create the group" business failure — the
latter should now always come from res.Error.Description).

=== MANDATORY RULES ===

CancellationToken forwarded on every I/O call touched.
No IMapper, no .Remove() introduced, no manual audit field mutation beyond
what Fix 2 explicitly requires for soft-delete reactivation.

content.text:
## Pre-task protocol

**Sections I will read:** Section 27 (Debug & Fix), Section 22 (Agent Operating Protocol), Section 26.6 (AuditableEntityInterceptor), Section 30.7 (multi-commit rule)

**Files I will MODIFY:**
- `Customer/Core/Application/Behaviors/ValidationBehavior.cs` (Fix 1)
- `Customer/Core/Application/Features/App/Chat/Users/Commands/BlockChatUser/BlockChatUserCommandHandler.cs` (Fix 2)
- `Customer/Api/EndPoint/Chat/CreateGroupEndPoint.cs` (Fix 3)
- `Customer/Api/EndPoint/Chat/CreateChannelEndPoint.cs` (Fix 3)
- `Customer/Api/EndPoint/Chat/SendChatMessageEndPoint.cs` (Fix 3)
- `Customer/Api/EndPoint/Chat/UploadChatFileEndPoint.cs` (Fix 3)

**Assumptions:**
- Admin `ValidationBehavior` is out of scope (unchanged)
- Strategy B (migration/filtered index) and `UnblockChatUserCommandHandler` are out of scope
- `DeleteBy`/`DeleteDate` must be cleared manually on reactivation (interceptor does not auto-clear them on `Modified`)
- `BlockedAt` stays `DateTime.UtcNow` to match existing chat handlers
- Fix 1+2 = one commit; Fix 3 = separate commit (not committed yet)

---

## Diff summary (6 files, +102 / −73)

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `ValidationBehavior.cs` | Replace broken `error as TResponse` with typed `Result` / `Result<T>` failure construction via reflection |
| `BlockChatUserCommandHandler.cs` | Query with `IgnoreQueryFilters()`; reactivate soft-deleted row or insert new |
| `CreateGroupEndPoint.cs` | Narrow try/catch to form read + avatar upload; MediatR path outside catch |
| `CreateChannelEndPoint.cs` | Same + preserve 409 conflict branch outside catch |
| `SendChatMessageEndPoint.cs` | Narrow try/catch to form parsing only |
| `UploadChatFileEndPoint.cs` | Narrow try/catch to form read only |

---

## Fix 1 approach

**Option 1 (reflection-based)** — no existing pattern found in Customer or Admin codebases (both had the same broken cast).

```csharp
if (typeof(TResponse) == typeof(Result))
    return (TResponse)(object)Result.Failure(validationError);

if (typeof(TResponse).IsGenericType &&
    typeof(TResponse).GetGenericTypeDefinition() == typeof(Result<>))
{
    return (TResponse)typeof(Result<>)
        .MakeGenericType(typeof(TResponse).GetGenericArguments()[0])
        .GetMethod(nameof(Result.Failure), new[] { typeof(Error) })!
        .Invoke(null, new object[] { validationError })!;
}
```

Uses the existing `Result<T>.Failure(Error)` factory rather than relying on compile-time implicit conversion.

---

## Fix 2 notes

- `IgnoreQueryFilters()` + tracked entity for reactivation
- Manual clear of `DeleteBy` / `DeleteDate` (interceptor only sets them on delete, never clears on undelete)
- `LastModifiedBy` / `LastModifiedDate` left to interceptor on `EntityState.Modified`

---

## Build confirmation

**`dotnet build Elay.sln`** — blocked by running Customer API (pid 13060) locking output DLLs (MSB3027/MSB3021 only, no CS errors).

**Clean compile verified:**
- `Customer/Core/Application/Application.csproj` → exit 0
- `Customer/Api/Api.csproj` → exit 0 (built to temp dir `$env:TEMP\elay-phase6-build`, no CS errors)

**Action before Newman:** stop the running API on `:5144`, then rebuild the solution.

---

## Deviations

None. Avatar upload in group/channel endpoints remains inside the narrowed try/catch (can throw I/O errors → `خطا در پردازش اطلاعات فرم`), which matches the prompt’s intent.

---

## Recommended commits (not created)

1. `fix(customer/app/chat): return typed validation failures and reactivate soft-deleted blocks`
2. `fix(customer/api/chat): narrow multipart catch blocks to form parsing only`

**Stopped here** — no commit, no Newman. Restart API with rebuilt binaries, then re-run Day 12 Newman to verify P02, B01, G02, G03, C03, M06.

# Phase 7 — Verification Report

## Step 1 — Clean Build Verification

| Check | Result |
|-------|--------|
| Stray `Api.exe` before build | **Found** — pid **13060** locking `Customer/Api/bin/Debug/net9.0/*.dll` |
| First `dotnet build Elay.sln` | **Failed** — MSB3027/MSB3021 file-lock errors only (no CS compile errors) |
| After stopping pid 13060 | **`Select-String " error "` → empty output** — build succeeded |
| API at `http://localhost:5144` | **`GET /health` → 200** (restarted via `dotnet run --project Customer/Api --launch-profile http` after build) |

**Note:** The developer’s pre-existing Api instance was still running at the start of this phase. It was stopped to get a clean build, then a fresh instance was started from this build for Newman.

---

## Step 2 — Newman Re-run

**Pre-run cleanup:**
- `DELETE /chat/users/2747/block` as sara → **400** (already unblocked — expected)
- Channel handle collision avoided via `.cursor/chat-day12-collection-v3-run.json` with unique handle `day12_ch_20260704203043`
- Conversation IDs recreated by collection flow (P01/P05 idempotent PV → id **4**; G01 → **10**; C01 → **11**)

**Full run:**
```powershell
npx newman run .cursor/chat-day12-collection-v3-run.json --reporters cli,json --reporter-json-export .cursor/chat-day12-run-results-v3.json
```
- **77 requests**, **0 request failures**
- **127 assertions**, **8 assertion failures** (pre-existing / unrelated — see below)
- Duration: **2m 38s**
- Results file: `.cursor/chat-day12-run-results-v3.json`

---

## Step 3 — Targeted Verification (3 Fixed Bugs + Regression)

# Phase 8 — Post-Commit Report

## Step 1 — N03 Sanity Check

**Verdict: CONFIRMED — unrelated to today's 3 fixes (not blocking)**

### Assertion logic (from collection)

**N02b** captures a baseline:
```javascript
const items = page.data || [];
pm.collectionVariables.set('notif_count_before_mute', String(items.length));
```
via `GET /Notification?page=0` as U2 (reza).

**N03** after unmute + send:
```javascript
const before = parseInt(pm.collectionVariables.get('notif_count_before_mute') || '0', 10);
pm.test('N03 new notification created', () => pm.expect(items.length).to.be.above(before));
pm.test('N03 references PV conversation', () => pm.expect(items.some(n => n.dataId === convId)).to.be.true);
```

Failure in v3: **`expected 10 to be above 10`** — page length stayed at 10, not that no notification was created.

### Why this is data/pagination pollution, not a regression

1. **Pagination cap:** `GetNotificationListQueryHandler` uses `.Pagination(request.Page)` with default **pageSize = 10**. When U2 already has ≥10 notifications on page 0, a new one does not increase `items.length` — the page still returns 10 rows.

2. **Notification was created:** In v3 Newman, **"N03 references PV conversation" passed** — the new notification is present; only the count comparison failed.

3. **Cross-run accumulation:** Notifications persist in DB for `chattest_reza`. Multiple Newman runs today (v2 + v3 + targeted probes) add notifications; v2 likely passed when `before` was 9 and `after` was 10.

4. **Today's fixes do not touch notification code:**
   - `SendChatMessageNotificationCommandHandler.cs` — **not modified** (last touched in `396bfd0`, chat module initial commit)
   - `ValidationBehavior.cs` — validation failure return path only
   - `BlockChatUserCommandHandler.cs` — block reactivation only
   - Endpoint catch narrowing — form parsing only; **N03b send returned 200**, so send path worked

**No evidence** that Fix 1/2/3 caused N03's failure.

---

| Api.exe locking DLLs | **Yes** — pid 27176 blocked initial build (stuck ~6 min) |
| After stopping Api + stuck build | **`Select-String " error "` → empty** (build clean) |

=== PHASE 10 — FIX 4 OF 7 KNOWN ISSUES (approved), DEFER 3 (documented reasoning) ===

Context: Day 12 is otherwise closed (2 commits landed: 28df10a, 617485c).
7 known issues were documented in docs/CHAT_KNOWN_ISSUES.md. After architect
review, here is the final decision per issue — do NOT deviate from this
scope without stopping and asking:

Issue 1 (PR03/M05 empty-body binder crash) → FIX NOW
Issue 2 (M06 wrong error message) → FIX NOW
Issue 3 (P04 wrong error message) → FIX NOW
Issue 4 (PG01/03/04 pageSize required) → INVESTIGATE FIRST, then fix
whichever side (API or test) is actually wrong
Issue 5 (N03 notification count via pagination) → DO NOT implement a new
feature. Documentation-only update (see Step 6).
Issue 6 (Unblock uses .Remove()) → FIX NOW, with explicit audit-field
handling since the automatic interceptor path will no longer apply
Issue 7 (filtered unique index / migration) → DO NOT implement any code
or migration. Only prepare the exact migration command text for human
review (see Step 7).
This continues to touch the Messaging domain, so before writing any fix
code, state your plan per GEMINI.md Section 22/27, then proceed — but you
do NOT need a separate stop-and-wait round for these specific 4 fixes since
architect approval is already given for the approach described below. If
during implementation you discover the actual code does NOT match the
described shape/hypothesis below for any of these issues, STOP and report
the discrepancy instead of improvising a different fix.

=== FIX 1 — Empty-body requests crash with HTML instead of JSON validation error ===

Affected endpoints: PUT /chat/profile (UpdateChatProfile), and the message
send endpoint (SendChatMessage) when called with a fully empty multipart
body (no fields at all, not even empty-string fields).

Read the FULL endpoint code for both UpdateChatProfileEndPoint (or wherever
PUT /chat/profile is mapped) and SendChatMessageEndPoint FIRST. Identify
exactly which parameter binding is currently REQUIRED (non-nullable,
without a default) that causes ASP.NET Core's minimal API model binder to
short-circuit with an HTML error page before the request ever reaches
MediatR's ValidationBehavior.

Fix: change the binding so every field that FluentValidation is supposed to
validate is bound as optional/nullable at the endpoint parameter level
(e.g. string? displayName = null, List<IFormFile>? attachments = null
or reading from IFormCollection/HttpRequest.Form defensively with
TryGetValue instead of a strongly-typed required binder), so that a
request with zero fields still successfully constructs a Command object
(with all-null/empty properties) and reaches the validator, which then
correctly returns the existing "حداقل یک فیلد برای ویرایش الزامی است" /
"پیام نمی‌تواند خالی باشد" messages instead of a raw ASP.NET exception.

Do not change any validator logic — only the endpoint's binding layer.
After the fix, verify with a manual PUT /chat/profile with zero form fields
and confirm you get a 400 JSON ProblemDetails with the existing validation
message, not an HTML exception page.

=== FIX 2 — M06: wrong error for message to non-existent conversation ===

Read SendChatMessageCommandHandler.cs IN FULL (already modified once today
for Fix 3 — re-read the CURRENT state, don't rely on memory of the earlier
version). Identify the exact order of checks: currently it likely queries
for the current user's ChatParticipant row directly (which naturally
returns null/not-found for a non-existent conversationId), producing
"شما عضو این مکالمه نیستید" (ChatErrors.NotAParticipant) even when the real
problem is the conversation doesn't exist at all.

Fix: add an explicit conversation-existence check BEFORE the participant
check:

csharp

var conversation = await context.ChatConversations
    .FirstOrDefaultAsync(c => c.Id == request.ConversationId, cancellationToken);

if (conversation is null)
    return ChatErrors.ConversationNotFound; // reuse existing error if already defined (confirm exact name from ChatErrors.cs — P04's evidence already showed "مکالمه یافت نشد" exists)

// existing participant check continues below, using the already-loaded conversation
Avoid introducing a duplicate query if the handler already loads the
conversation object elsewhere for a different purpose (e.g. the PV-block
check added in an earlier fix today) — reuse that existing query result if
present; read the full current handler first to confirm before adding a
new query.

=== FIX 3 — P04: wrong error for PV creation targeting non-existent user ===

Read CreateOrGetPvConversationCommandHandler.cs IN FULL (already has the
self-chat guard from earlier today). Currently, when TargetUserId refers to
a non-existent AppUser, the handler falls through to some later check that
produces "مکالمه یافت نشد" (ChatErrors.ConversationNotFound), which is
misleading since no conversation was ever expected.

Fix: add an explicit target-user-existence check right after the self-chat
guard, before any conversation lookup/creation logic:

csharp

var targetUserExists = await context.AppUsers
    .AnyAsync(u => u.Id == request.TargetUserId, cancellationToken);

if (!targetUserExists)
    return ChatErrors.TargetUserNotFound;
Add the new error to ChatErrors.cs, matching the exact existing style
(Persian description, appropriate ErrorType — likely NotFound, check how
other NotFound-style errors in this file are typed):

csharp

public static Error TargetUserNotFound =>
    Error.NotFound("Chat.TargetUserNotFound", "کاربر مورد نظر یافت نشد");
(Confirm the exact Error.NotFound/Error.Failure factory signature pattern
used elsewhere in ChatErrors.cs before writing this — match it exactly.)

=== FIX 4 — PG01/PG03/PG04: pageSize incorrectly required ===

First, INVESTIGATE (do not assume my hypothesis is correct without
checking): read the GetChatMessagesEndPoint (or wherever
GET /chat/conversations/{id}/messages is mapped) IN FULL, and the
GetChatMessagesQuery record definition. Confirm:

The record's default is PageSize = 20 (per earlier discovery).
Whether the endpoint's lambda parameter for pageSize has ITS OWN
default value matching the record (e.g. int pageSize = 20 in the
app.MapGet(...) delegate signature), or whether it's declared without
a default (e.g. just int pageSize), which would force ASP.NET Core's
minimal API binder to treat it as a REQUIRED query parameter regardless
of the record's own default.
If the endpoint parameter lacks a default value matching the record: this
confirms my hypothesis — FIX by adding int pageSize = 20 (or whatever the
record's actual default is) directly in the endpoint's parameter list, so
omitting ?pageSize= in the query string no longer 400s.

If investigation reveals a DIFFERENT root cause (e.g. the parameter is
already optional in code but something else causes the 400, such as a
route-level required attribute, or the Postman collection's query building
was actually wrong despite the earlier Phase 4b fix): report the real cause
and fix whichever side (API code vs Postman collection test) is actually
broken — do not force a code change if the collection is what's wrong.

=== FIX 5 — Documentation-only update for N03 (do NOT write feature code) ===

Do not implement any new endpoint or count field. Only update
docs/CHAT_KNOWN_ISSUES.md's Issue 5 entry to explicitly state:
"Decision: not implementing a dedicated unread-count endpoint at this time
— out of scope as a bug fix, would be a new feature. Frontend guidance:
rely on the SignalR ConversationUpdated event's unreadCount field for

=== FIX 6 — Unblock: replace .Remove() with explicit soft-delete ===

Read UnblockChatUserCommandHandler.cs IN FULL, and re-read

# Chat Module — Known Issues (Day 12)

Tracking document for Newman/integration-test findings and deferred backend work.
Last updated: Phase 10 (2026-07-04).

---

## Issue 1 — PR03 / M05: Empty-body binder crash (HTML instead of JSON)

**Symptom:** `PUT /chat/profile` with zero form fields, or `POST .../messages` with an
empty multipart body, returned an ASP.NET HTML `BadHttpRequestException` page instead
of a JSON `400 ProblemDetails` validation message.

**Root cause:** Minimal API `[FromForm]` parameter binding short-circuited before
MediatR/FluentValidation when the multipart body had no parts.

**Status:** **Fixed (Phase 10)** — endpoints now read `HttpContext.Request.Form`
defensively and always construct commands with null/empty values so validators run.

---

## Issue 2 — M06: Wrong error for message to non-existent conversation

**Symptom:** `POST /chat/conversations/999999/messages` returned
`"شما عضو این مکالمه نیستید"` (`ChatErrors.NotAParticipant`) instead of
`"مکالمه یافت نشد"` (`ChatErrors.ConversationNotFound`).

**Root cause:** `SendChatMessageCommandHandler` checked participant membership
before verifying the conversation exists.

**Status:** **Fixed (Phase 10)** — conversation existence is checked first.

---

## Issue 3 — P04: Wrong error for PV creation targeting non-existent user

**Symptom:** `POST /chat/conversations/pv/999999` returned
`ChatErrors.ConversationNotFound` instead of a user-not-found message.

**Root cause:** `CreateOrGetPvConversationCommandHandler` mapped missing/inactive
target users to `ConversationNotFound`.

**Status:** **Fixed (Phase 10)** — returns new `ChatErrors.TargetUserNotFound`
(`"کاربر مورد نظر یافت نشد"`).

---

## Issue 4 — PG01 / PG03 / PG04: `pageSize` incorrectly required

**Symptom:** `GET /chat/conversations/{id}/messages` without `?pageSize=` returned
`400` with `Required parameter "int pageSize"`.

**Root cause:** `GetChatMessagesEndPoint` declared `[FromQuery] int pageSize`
without a default value, so ASP.NET Core treated it as required even though
`GetChatMessagesQuery` defaults `PageSize` to 20.

**Status:** **Fixed (Phase 10)** — endpoint parameter now `int pageSize = 20`.

---

## Issue 5 — N03: Notification count assertion via paginated list length

**Symptom:** Newman test `N03 - New Notification After Unmute` failed with
`expected 10 to be above 10` when U2 already had a full first page (10 items) of
notifications from prior runs.

**Root cause:** Test compares `GET /Notification?page=0` **page item count**
(`items.length`), not total unread count. Pagination caps page size at 10, so a
new notification does not increase `items.length` once the page is full.

**Decision:** **Not implementing a dedicated unread-count endpoint at this time**
— out of scope as a bug fix; would be a new feature.

**Frontend guidance:**
- Rely on the SignalR `ConversationUpdated` event's `unreadCount` field for
  real-time unread tracking.
- Do **not** use `GET /Notification` paginated list length as an unread-count
  proxy.
- Do **not** use `GET /chat/conversations` list length as an unread-count proxy.

See also `docs/CHAT_API_DOCUMENTATION.md` §9.6 (Notifications ↔ mute interaction).

**Status:** **Documented only — no code change.**

---

## Issue 6 — Unblock uses `.Remove()` (soft-delete via interceptor)

**Symptom:** `UnblockChatUserCommandHandler` called `context.ChatBlocks.Remove()`,
which triggered `AuditableEntityInterceptor`'s `EntityState.Deleted` branch
(soft-delete). Combined with an unfiltered unique index, this created ghost rows
that broke re-block (fixed separately in commit `28df10a` via reactivation).

**Root cause:** `.Remove()` relied on interceptor soft-delete instead of explicit
field assignment; inconsistent with other manual soft-deletes in the codebase.

**Status:** **Fixed (Phase 10)** — explicit `IsDeleted = true` with manual
`DeleteBy` / `DeleteDate` (interceptor `Modified` branch does not populate delete
audit fields). `BlockChatUserCommandHandler` reactivation logic unchanged.

---

## Issue 7 — Filtered unique index on `ChatBlocks` (deferred migration)

**Symptom:** Soft-deleted `(BlockerUserId, BlockedUserId)` rows remain in the table
with a non-filtered unique index `IX_Chat_Blocks_BlockerUserId_BlockedUserId`,
requiring reactivation logic to avoid duplicate-key errors on re-block.

**Decision:** **Deferred** — no migration or schema change in Phase 10. Reactivation
in `BlockChatUserCommandHandler` (commit `28df10a`) is the current mitigation.

When a human decides to apply a filtered unique index, run:

```text
dotnet ef migrations add AddFilteredUniqueIndexToChatBlocks \
  --project Customer/Infrastructure \
  --startup-project Customer/Api \
  --context ApplicationDbContext \
  --output-dir Migrations
```

⚠️ **MIGRATION REQUIRED — not created in Phase 10, requires human review per
GEMINI.md Section 26.** Review the generated migration before applying to the database.

**Status:** **Documentation only — no code or migration file created.**

---

## Previously fixed (Day 12 Phase 6 — commits `28df10a`, `617485c`)

| Bug | Fix |
|-----|-----|
| P02 / G02 / G03 / C03 validation masked as 500 or generic errors | `ValidationBehavior` typed failure + narrowed multipart catch blocks |
| B01 ghost-row duplicate key on re-block | `BlockChatUserCommandHandler` reactivates soft-deleted rows |

input.new_string:
The frontend's own "new message" banner/toast (Phase 9 of the implementation
plan) should be driven by the `ReceiveMessage` event plus the conversation's
client-known `isMuted` flag — do not rely on `NotificationCenter` entries for the
in-chat real-time banner, since that mechanism already excludes muted
conversations and forwarded messages inconsistently with what actually arrives
live.

**Unread count guidance (Day 12 known issue N03):** Do not use `GET /Notification`
paginated list length as an unread-count proxy — the endpoint returns at most one
page of notification centers and the item count does not increase once the page
is full. For real-time unread badges, rely on the SignalR `ConversationUpdated`
event's `unreadCount` field instead. A dedicated unread-count REST endpoint is
**not** planned as part of the current chat bug-fix scope.

$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
$baseUrl = 'http://localhost:5144'

function Login-User($username) {
    $body = @{
        username = $username
        password = 'Test@12345'
        deviceInfo = @{
            deviceID = 'BE2A.250530.026.F3'
            deviceName = 'phase10-verify'
            deviceVersion = '16'
            appVersion = '2.113.0'
            platform = 'android'
        }
    } | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 3
    return (Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "$baseUrl/Auth/Login" -Method POST -ContentType 'application/json' -Body $body).accessToken
}

function Curl-Check($label, $args) {
    Write-Host "`n=== $label ==="
    $out = & curl.exe -s -w "`nHTTP:%{http_code}" @args
    Write-Host $out
}

$token = Login-User 'chattest_sara'

# Fix 1 PR03 - empty multipart PUT profile
Curl-Check 'PR03 empty profile form' @(
    '-X','PUT',"$baseUrl/chat/profile",
    '-H',"Authorization: Bearer $token",
    '-F','displayName=',
    '-F','avatar='
)
# Also truly empty multipart (boundary only)
Curl-Check 'PR03 empty profile (no fields)' @(
    '-X','PUT',"$baseUrl/chat/profile",
    '-H',"Authorization: Bearer $token",
    '-H','Content-Type: multipart/form-data; boundary=----empty',
    '--data-binary',"------empty--`r`n"
)

# Fix 1 M05 - empty multipart message (need valid conv id - get from PV create)
$pv = Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "$baseUrl/chat/conversations/pv/2747" -Method POST -Headers @{ Authorization = "Bearer $token" }
$convId = $pv.id
Curl-Check 'M05 empty message form' @(
    '-X','POST',"$baseUrl/chat/conversations/$convId/messages",
    '-H',"Authorization: Bearer $token",
    '-H','Content-Type: multipart/form-data; boundary=----empty',
    '--data-binary',"------empty--`r`n"
)

# Fix 2 M06
Curl-Check 'M06 nonexistent conversation' @(
    '-X','POST',"$baseUrl/chat/conversations/999999/messages",
    '-H',"Authorization: Bearer $token",
    '-F','text=ghost'
)

# Fix 3 P04
Curl-Check 'P04 nonexistent user PV' @(
    '-X','POST',"$baseUrl/chat/conversations/pv/999999",
    '-H',"Authorization: Bearer $token"
)

# Fix 4 PG01 - no pageSize
Curl-Check 'PG01 no pageSize param' @(
    '-X','GET',"$baseUrl/chat/conversations/$convId/messages",
    '-H',"Authorization: Bearer $token"
)

# Fix 6 block cycle
Write-Host "`n=== B01 block/unblock/re-block cycle ==="
foreach ($step in @('block1','unblock','block2')) {
    if ($step -match 'block') {
        $r = curl.exe -s -w "`nHTTP:%{http_code}" -X POST "$baseUrl/chat/users/2747/block" -H "Authorization: Bearer $token"
    } else {
        $r = curl.exe -s -w "`nHTTP:%{http_code}" -X DELETE "$baseUrl/chat/users/2747/block" -H "Authorization: Bearer $token"
    }
    Write-Host "$step : $r"
}
curl.exe -s -X DELETE "$baseUrl/chat/conversations/2747/block" -H "Authorization: Bearer $token" | Out-Null
curl.exe -s -X DELETE "$baseUrl/chat/users/2747/block" -H "Authorization: Bearer $token" | Out-Null

content.text:
# Phase 10 Report — Known Issues Fixes (Not Committed)

## Plan (Sections 22/27)

**Files modified (code):**
- `UpdateChatProfileEndPoint.cs`, `SendChatMessageEndPoint.cs` — Fix 1 binding
- `SendChatMessageCommandHandler.cs` — Fix 2 conversation-first check
- `CreateOrGetPvConversationCommandHandler.cs`, `ChatErrors.cs` — Fix 3 `TargetUserNotFound`
- `GetChatMessagesEndPoint.cs` — Fix 4 optional `pageSize`
- `UnblockChatUserCommandHandler.cs` — Fix 6 explicit soft-delete

**Files created/modified (docs only):**
- `docs/CHAT_KNOWN_ISSUES.md` (new)
- `docs/CHAT_API_DOCUMENTATION.md` (N03 guidance in §9.6)

**Not touched:** `SendChatMessageNotificationCommandHandler.cs`, Admin context, migrations.

---

## N03 Sanity Check — **CONFIRMED unrelated**

| Evidence | Detail |
|----------|--------|
| Assertion logic | N02b stores `notif_count_before_mute = items.length` from `GET /Notification?page=0`; N03 asserts `items.length > before` |
| Failure mode | `expected 10 to be above 10` — page already full (default page size 10) |
| Persistence | Notifications accumulate across Newman runs for `chattest_reza`; not reset between runs |
| Today's fixes | None touch notification create/query/count paths; `SendChatMessageNotificationCommandHandler.cs` unchanged |
| v4 Newman | N03 still fails; **"N03 references PV conversation" passes** — notification exists, count comparison doesn't grow |

**Verdict:** Flaky test design + cumulative DB data. **Not caused by Fix 1/2/3.**

---

## Fix 4 Root Cause — **Hypothesis CONFIRMED (with adjustment)**

**Finding:** `GetChatMessagesEndPoint` had `[FromQuery] int pageSize` with **no default**, so ASP.NET Core treated it as **required** despite `GetChatMessagesQuery` defaulting to 20.

**Fix applied:** `[FromQuery] int? pageSize` + `pageSize is null or <= 0 ? 20 : pageSize.Value`  
(Could not use `int pageSize = 20` in the lambda — **CS1737**: optional params cannot precede required `ISender`/`CancellationToken`.)

---

## Diff Summary

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `UpdateChatProfileEndPoint.cs` | `HttpContext` form read; empty/invalid form → validator (not HTML crash) |
| `SendChatMessageEndPoint.cs` | Removed `[FromForm]` binders; defensive form read; empty → validator |
| `GetChatMessagesEndPoint.cs` | `pageSize` optional (`int?`), defaults to 20 |
| `SendChatMessageCommandHandler.cs` | Conversation existence check **before** participant check |
| `CreateOrGetPvConversationCommandHandler.cs` | Returns `TargetUserNotFound` for missing user |
| `ChatErrors.cs` | Added `TargetUserNotFound` |
| `UnblockChatUserCommandHandler.cs` | Replaced `.Remove()` with explicit `IsDeleted` + `DeleteBy` + `DeleteDate` |
| `docs/CHAT_KNOWN_ISSUES.md` | All 7 issues documented (Issues 5 & 7 doc-only) |
| `docs/CHAT_API_DOCUMENTATION.md` | N03 unread-count guidance in §9.6 |

**Fix 6 audit verification:** Interceptor only sets `DeleteBy`/`DeleteDate` on `EntityState.Deleted`. Manual soft-delete via `Modified` requires manual fields — matches `MessageDeleteCommandHandler` pattern (`Username()` + `DateTime.Now`). `BlockChatUserCommandHandler` reactivation (`IsDeleted` check) **unchanged and compatible**.

---

## Build

```
dotnet build Elay.sln 2>&1 | Select-String " error "
→ empty (after stopping locking Api.exe)
```

---

## Live Verification (6 checks)

| Check | Result |
|-------|--------|
| PR03 empty PUT `/chat/profile` | **400 JSON** `"حداقل یک فیلد برای ویرایش الزامی است"` |
| M05 empty POST message | **400 JSON** `"پیام نمی‌تواند خالی باشد"` |
| M06 POST conv 999999 | **400** `"مکالمه یافت نشد"` |
| P04 POST pv/999999 | **400** `"کاربر مورد نظر یافت نشد"` |
| PG01 GET messages (no pageSize) | **200** with messages array |
| Block → unblock → re-block | **200 / 200 / 200** |

---

## Newman Regression (v3 → v4)

| Metric | v3 (post Phase 6) | v4 (post Phase 10) |
|--------|-------------------|---------------------|
| **Covered** | **48** | **54** (+6) |
| **Failed (known)** | **7** | **1** (N03 only) |
| Assertions | 127 pass / 8 fail | 128 pass / 1 fail |

**Newly Covered:** PR03, M05, M06, PG01, PG03, PG04

**Still Failed:** N03 only (documented, not blocking)

**Note:** v4 run used a local collection copy with P04 assertion updated to expect `"کاربر مورد نظر یافت نشد"` (intentional behavior change). The repo's `.cursor/chat-day12-collection.json` still asserts the old P04 message — update separately if you want Newman green without a patched copy.

Results: `.cursor/chat-day12-run-results-v4.json`

---

## Issues 5 & 7 — Documentation Only

| Issue | Action |
|-------|--------|
| **5 (N03)** | Documented in `CHAT_KNOWN_ISSUES.md` + `CHAT_API_DOCUMENTATION.md` — no feature code |
| **7 (filtered index)** | Deferred; exact `dotnet ef migrations add AddFilteredUniqueIndexToChatBlocks ...` command documented with ⚠️ human-review flag — **no migration file created** |

---

## Proposed Commit(s) — **awaiting your approval**

**Recommended: 2 commits** (code vs docs per Section 30.7)

**Commit 1 — code fixes:**
```powershell
rtk git add Customer/Api/EndPoint/Chat/UpdateChatProfileEndPoint.cs
rtk git add Customer/Api/EndPoint/Chat/SendChatMessageEndPoint.cs
rtk git add Customer/Api/EndPoint/Chat/GetChatMessagesEndPoint.cs
rtk git add Customer/Core/Application/Errors/ChatErrors.cs
rtk git add Customer/Core/Application/Features/App/Chat/Conversations/Commands/CreateOrGetPvConversation/CreateOrGetPvConversationCommandHandler.cs
rtk git add Customer/Core/Application/Features/App/Chat/Messages/Commands/SendChatMessage/SendChatMessageCommandHandler.cs
rtk git add Customer/Core/Application/Features/App/Chat/Users/Commands/UnblockChatUser/UnblockChatUserCommandHandler.cs
rtk git commit -m "fix(customer/app+api/chat): resolve Day 12 known validation and error-message issues" -m "Empty multipart bodies reach FluentValidation (PR03/M05). M06/P04 return correct errors. GET messages pageSize optional (default 20). Unblock uses explicit soft-delete with audit fields."
```

**Commit 2 — documentation:**
```powershell
rtk git add docs/CHAT_KNOWN_ISSUES.md docs/CHAT_API_DOCUMENTATION.md
rtk git commit -m "docs(customer/chat): document known issues and N03 unread-count guidance"
```

**Exclude:** `launchSettings.json`, `.cursor/*`, `mcp.json`

---

## Day 12 Final Tally

| Status | Count |
|--------|-------|
| **Covered** | **54** |
| **Failed (known, not blocking)** | **1** (N03) |
| **Blocked** | **0** |

No code beyond approved scope was committed. Waiting for your go-ahead to commit.

content.text:
# Day 12 Close-out Report — Phase 11 Complete

## Step 1 — Collection sync + Newman (confirmed)

`.cursor/chat-day12-collection.json` was updated so **P04** expects `"کاربر مورد نظر یافت نشد"`. A full-file search shows `"مکالمه یافت نشد"` only on **M06** (correct — different scenario).

**Final Newman run** (`.cursor/chat-day12-run-results-final.json`):

| Metric | Count |
|--------|-------|
| **Covered** | **54** |
| **Failed** | **1** (N03 only) |
| **Blocked** | **0** |

Matches Phase 10 v4. The sole failure: `N03 new notification created` — `expected 10 to be above 10` (documented known issue).

---

## Step 2 — Commit 1 (code fixes)

Build check passed (no errors). Exactly **7 files** committed:

**`b3eff47`** — `fix(customer/app+api/chat): resolve Day 12 known validation and error-message issues`

---

## Step 3 — Commit 2 (documentation)

Exactly **2 files** committed:

**`688a8c5`** — `docs(customer/chat): document known issues and N03 unread-count guidance`

**Not included in either commit:** `launchSettings.json`, `.cursor/mcp.json`, `.cursor/*` tooling files.

---

## Step 4 — Today's 4 commits (in order)

| # | Hash | Message |
|---|------|---------|
| 1 | `28df10a` | `fix(customer/app/chat): return typed validation failures and reactivate soft-deleted blocks` |
| 2 | `617485c` | `fix(customer/api/chat): narrow multipart catch blocks to form parsing only` |
| 3 | `b3eff47` | `fix(customer/app+api/chat): resolve Day 12 known validation and error-message issues` |
| 4 | `688a8c5` | `docs(customer/chat): document known issues and N03 unread-count guidance` |

Branch `feature/chat-module` is **ahead 4** of `origin/feature/chat-module`. Nothing was pushed.

---

## Step 5 — Working tree status

**Modified (unstaged, intentional local-only):**
- `Customer/Api/Properties/launchSettings.json`
- `.cursor/mcp.json`

**Untracked (local tooling / test artifacts):**
- `.cursor/*` (collection, Newman results, scripts, test-user helper)
- `docs/CHAT_FRONTEND_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md`

No unintended staged changes. Source and docs from today’s work are fully committed.

---

## Stability statement

The Chat module backend is **stable and ready for frontend integration**. All 55 Day 12 scenario contracts are addressed in Newman: **54 Covered**, with the single remaining failure **N03** (notification list pagination cap — unread count does not increase when the page is already full at 10 items). That issue is documented in `docs/CHAT_KNOWN_ISSUES.md` and is **non-blocking** for frontend work; clients should use conversation-level unread APIs rather than relying on notification list length growth.

Day 12 close-out is complete. Push to remote remains a separate, developer-approved action.
